title: Experiment FSW
description: This experiment will show what happens if you combine three optimization assemblies
body:
![This project is just an experiment, please do not take it seriously](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6828c38504baa003f001ff62f9b0f2dbee8e183b.png)

![Fabulously Optimized](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0c53a964aee4757d9612242f70993b3c8ed09196.png)
![Simply Optimized](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a93e984389bc1ecddf1579f7280bbc79cc996ab5.png)
![wing](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0dd8a390e4bca079a695c7d551d3e55f60a608bf.png)
![Experiment FSW](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/05793d591eed7d9b6142a1bfbc589f15dbd83377.png)

<details>
<summary>🤔 What is this?</summary>

**Experiment FSW** is a project that was created in order to answer a fairly common question: “What happens if you combine three optimization assemblies into one?”

</details>

<details>
<summary>🤨 How does it work?</summary>

The modpack combines three optimization assemblies, namely: Fabulously Optimized, Simply Optimized and Wing. Many people think this will improve game performance by 3 times, but is it true? Download the modpack and get the answer to this question

![е](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/1a3a919853bc6a5bd652a40c60f40e18fa7564bf.png)

</details>



<details>
<summary>⚡ What versions of modpacks do you use?</summary>

**Fabulously Optimized:**
- 1.20.1 - 5.4.1 Release

**Simply Optimized**
- 1.20.1 - 11.3 Release

**Wing**
- 1.20.1 - 5.38 Release

</details>


<details>
<summary>🌊 What are experimental modpacks from Octagonal studio?</summary>

Experimental modpacks are forgotten quite quickly and stop updating. They are needed so that users can find out the answers to some questions of the “What will happen if...” format.

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔗 Links</summary>

- [Fabulously Optimized](https://modrinth.com/modpack/fabulously-optimized)
- [Simply Optimized](https://modrinth.com/modpack/sop)
- [Wing](https://modrinth.com/modpack/wing)

</details>
